original_hypothesis.title: mintWithBudget Permanently Non-Functional After Generation 1
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: mintWithBudget uses `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` as its while-loop condition. _tokenIds is a global monotonically increasing counter, while maxTokensPerGen is 10,000. After generation 1 completes (10,000 tokens), _tokenIds >= 10,000 and the loop never executes. mintWithBudget becomes permanently non-functional for all subsequent generations, refunding all ETH.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The loop condition compares a global counter (_tokenIds) against a per-generation limit (maxTokensPerGen), which is a clear logic error. The condition should use generationMintCounts[currentGeneration].

proof_of_signal.pattern: while (budgetLeft >= mintPrice && _tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen) uses global _tokenIds instead of generationMintCounts[currentGeneration]

preconditions: _tokenIds >= maxTokensPerGen (10,000 total tokens minted across all generations)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Global counter compared to per-generation limit
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: _tokenIds is never reset between generations; maxTokensPerGen = 10000

analysis_questions: Does any other path compensate for this bug?
What percentage of users would use mintWithBudget vs mintToken?
validation_requirements: Test mintWithBudget in gen 2+ returns all ETH as refund
Verify the loop body never executes

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: No attacker action needed. After gen 1 fills, any user calling mintWithBudget has their entire msg.value refunded because the while loop condition (_tokenIds < 10000) is always false. Batch minting is permanently disabled for gen 2+.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of batch minting functionality; users must mint one at a time via mintToken, degrading UX significantly
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users trying to batch-mint in generations 2-10

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for mintWithBudget in generation 2+

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable immediately when users try batch minting in gen 2

validation_result.mechanism: Global counter vs per-generation limit comparison
validation_result.impact_summary: mintWithBudget becomes permanently non-functional after generation 1 completes because the while-loop condition compares a global monotonically increasing counter (_tokenIds) against a per-generation limit (maxTokensPerGen=10000), which is always false once _tokenIds >= 10000.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The mintWithBudget function at TraitForgeNft.sol line 202 uses `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` as part of its while-loop condition. `_tokenIds` is a private uint256 counter that starts at 0 and is incremented by 1 in both `_mintInternal` (line 267: `_tokenIds++`) and `_mintNewEntity` (line 290: `_tokenIds++`). It is never reset or decremented anywhere in the contract. `maxTokensPerGen` is set to 10000. After generation 1 is fully minted (10,000 tokens via minting, potentially more from forging), `_tokenIds` will be >= 10000. The condition `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` (i.e., `10000 < 10000`) evaluates to `false`, so the while loop body never executes. All ETH sent is refunded. The correct condition should use `generationMintCounts[currentGeneration] < maxTokensPerGen`, which is what `_mintInternal` uses internally (line 265) to check generation boundaries. Notably, `mintToken` does NOT have this bug because it doesn't use a while loop — it calls `_mintInternal` directly, which handles generation increments correctly.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof-of-signal is accurate. TraitForgeNft.sol line 202 contains `while (budgetLeft >= mintPrice && _tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen)`. The `_tokenIds` variable is incremented at line 267 (`_tokenIds++`) in `_mintInternal` and at line 290 in `_mintNewEntity`, and is never reset. After 10,000 mints across all generations, `_tokenIds >= 10000` permanently, making the loop condition always false for gen 2+. Meanwhile, `mintToken` (line 172) works fine because it calls `_mintInternal` directly without the faulty loop guard.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The while-loop condition `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` compares a global token counter (which only increases and is never reset) against a per-generation limit of 10,000. After generation 1 completes, _tokenIds >= 10000 permanently, so this condition is always false and the loop never executes for any subsequent generation. The correct check should be `generationMintCounts[currentGeneration] < maxTokensPerGen`.

_mintInternal correctly uses generationMintCounts[currentGeneration] to check generation limits at line 265, but mintWithBudget's loop guard uses _tokenIds instead. The _tokenIds++ at line 267 shows this counter only increases, never resets between generations.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls mintWithBudget with ETH and proof after gen 1 is complete
2. calculateMintPrice() returns valid price for gen 2 (line 197)
3. While loop condition evaluated: budgetLeft >= mintPrice (TRUE) && _tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen (FALSE since _tokenIds=10000, maxTokensPerGen=10000)
5. budgetLeft == msg.value > 0, so full refund is sent to caller (line 206)
6. Function completes without minting any tokens
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: After generation 1 completes (10,000 tokens minted via mintToken or mintWithBudget), _tokenIds reaches 10,000 (or higher if forges occurred). When any user calls mintWithBudget for generation 2+, the while-loop at line 202 evaluates `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` which is `10000 < 10000 = false`. The loop body is skipped entirely. The budgetLeft equals msg.value, so the entire payment is refunded at line 206-208. No tokens are minted. This persists permanently for all future generations.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts in deployTasks.ts show standard deployment with no special maxTokensPerGen configuration. The default value of 10000 is used. No migration or workaround for this bug is present in deployment scripts.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: No attacker action is required. This is a latent logic bug that activates automatically after generation 1 is fully minted. Any user calling mintWithBudget in generation 2 or later receives a complete ETH refund because the loop condition `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` evaluates to false. The function becomes a no-op that wastes gas. Users must fall back to single-token minting via mintToken, which does not have this bug because it calls _mintInternal directly without the faulty loop guard.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Batch minting functionality (mintWithBudget) is permanently disabled after generation 1 completes. Users in generations 2-10 must mint one token at a time via mintToken, significantly degrading UX and increasing gas costs for users who want to mint multiple tokens. This affects 90% of the protocol's lifetime (generations 2-10 out of 10 total). No workaround exists without contract redeployment or migration.
recommended_mitigations: Replace `_tokenIds < maxTokensPerGen` with `generationMintCounts[currentGeneration] < maxTokensPerGen` in the while-loop condition of mintWithBudget
Add comprehensive tests for mintWithBudget across generation boundaries, specifically testing behavior in generations 2+
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy all contracts via deploy-all task. 2. Write entropy batches. 3. Mint 10,000 tokens in generation 1 using mintToken to fill gen 1. 4. Verify currentGeneration is now 2 and _tokenIds >= 10000. 5. Call mintWithBudget with sufficient ETH for gen 2. 6. Verify that 0 tokens are minted and all ETH is refunded. 7. Call mintToken with same ETH to verify single minting still works in gen 2.
uncertainties: The exact _tokenIds value after gen 1 depends on whether any forge-minted tokens occurred (which also increment _tokenIds). However, forging only makes _tokenIds higher, making the bug trigger even more certainly.
Note: INV-STATE-001 (initializeAlphaIndices onlyOwner revert) may prevent generation increment entirely, which would make this bug moot — but both are independent code defects.
additional_testing_needed: Test mintWithBudget in generation 2 after gen 1 is fully minted
Test mintWithBudget behavior when forge-minted tokens exist (which increase _tokenIds beyond 10000 even faster)
Integration test covering full generation 1 -> generation 2 transition with both mintToken and mintWithBudget
